did.unittitle: Merrill Joan Gerber papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 101, 102, 151 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 152 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ken Lopez Bookseller on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Writings, 1945-2016. II. Professional correspondence and papers, 1932-2017. III. Personal and family papers, 1895-2016. IV. Printed material, 1956-2017.

After receipt the writings, professional correspondence and papers, and personal and family papers were roughly sorted into series by box, but within individual boxes papers were generally kept in the order in which they were received. As a result, some materials in Series I, II, and III remain intermixed.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Merrill Joan Gerber Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: This collection contains papers concerning the work, life, and family of author Merrill Joan Gerber. Extensive manuscript and typescript drafts, proofs, notes, research, correspondence, and other related papers document Gerber's short stories, poetry, and novels, including Now Molly Knows (Arbor House, 1971), Please Don't Kiss Me Now (Dial Press, 1981), The Kingdom of Brooklyn (Longstreet Press, 1992), Glimmering Girls (University of Wisconsin Press, Terrace Books, 2005), and The Hysterectomy Waltz (Dzanc Books, 2013), among many others. Literary correspondence, publishers correspondence, calendars, conference papers, teaching materials, book publicity materials, grant applications, books reviews, audiovisual materials, computer media, and other professional papers document Gerber's professional life as a both a writer and an educator. Significant literary correspondents include Cyrilly Abels, Norma Klein, Cynthia Ozick, Lynne Sharon Schwarz, Wallace Stegner, Mary Ann Taylor-Hall, Arturo Vivante, and Charlotte Zoë Walker. Family correspondence, writings of family members, scrapbooks, diaries, notebooks, photographs, yearbooks, and other personal papers document Gerber's family life and history. Printed materials include magazines, journals, and other serials containing contributions by Gerber.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Merrill Joan Gerber Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

bioghist.head: Merrill Joan Gerber
bioghist.p: Merril Joan Gerber is an American novelist and short story writer. She was born in 1938 in Brooklyn, New York, to William and Jessie Gerber. She has published over thirty books of fiction, young adult fiction, and memoir, including Now Molly Knows (Arbor House, 1971), Please Don't Kiss Me Now (Dial Press, 1981), The Kingdom of Brooklyn (Longstreet Press, 1992), and Glimmering Girls (University of Wisconsin Press, Terrace Books, 2005). Gerber's writing has been recognized wtih a Wallace Stegner Fiction Fellowship, the Harold Ribalow Prize, and the Pushcart Editors' Book Award. She married Joseph Spiro in 1960, with whom she has three daughters.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

did.unittitle: Writings

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Within this series, the papers are roughly in the order in which they were received. Minimal re-arrangement was performed within individual boxes. Writings can also be found within Series II. Professional correspondence and paprers and Series III. Personal and family papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings consist of extensive manuscript and typescript drafts, proofs, notes, research, correspondence, and other related papers documenting Merrill Joan Gerber's short stories, poetry, and novels, including Now Molly Knows (Arbor House, 1971), Please Don't Kiss Me Now (Dial Press, 1981), The Kingdom of Brooklyn (Longstreet Press, 1992), Glimmering Girls (University of Wisconsin Press, Terrace Books, 2005), and The Hysterectomy Waltz (Dzanc Books, 2013), among many others.

Professional correspondence and papers

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Within this series, the papers are roughly in the order in which they were received. Minimal re-arrangement was performed within individual boxes. Professional correspondence and papers can also be found within Series I. Writings and Series III. Personal and family papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professional correspondence and papers consist of titerary correspondence, publishers correspondence, calendars, conference papers, teaching materials, book publicity materials, grant applications, book reviews, audiovisual materials, computer media, and other professional papers documenting Merrill Joan Gerber's professional life as a both a writer and an educator. Significant literary correspondents include Cyrilly Abels, Norma Klein, Cynthia Ozick, Lynne Sharon Schwarz, Wallace Stegner, Mary Ann Taylor-Hall, Arturo Vivante, and Charlotte Zoë Walker.

did.unittitle: Merrill Joan Gerber term papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts of "Music as a Function of Society," "Brief Analysis of the Prioress's Tale by Chaucer," "A Brief Analysis of Emma Bovary," "Critique," "Flaubert's 'Madame Bovary,'" "Neo-Classicism in Literature," "Critique on Tobias Smollett's 'The Expedition of Humphry Clinker,'" "Papa Leiro," "Observation of Reversals in Behavior Patters During a Crowd Situation," "The Bridge of Adolescence," "Status, Expectation, and Trait as Observed in a Relationship between A and B," "A Writer's Environment Reflected in His Writings," "Wedding Rings and Scholarly Things," "The Personalities of Rosseau and Thoreau Compared and Contrasted," "Olde and Middle English," "The Significance of the Song of Solomon," "Dostoevsky: Craftsman Rather Than Artist," "The Overprotected Child in American Society," "The English Language: Its Form," "Sir John Vanbrugh: Architect and Dramatist," "John Dennis: Critic," "Offensive Elements in the Violence of Don Quixote," "The Influence of Moliere on Wycherly," "Corneille's Influence on Dryden as Evidenced in the Essay of Dramatic Poesy," "The Theory of Heroic Drama," "Le Rouge et Le Noir," "Biography and the Kindly Giant: A Paper on Samuel Johnson," "If Your Child Hates His Name--Are You to Blame?," "Must Students Lie to Get Summer Jobs?," "The Literary Career of Emily Dickinson," and "Free Lance Writing."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Within this series, the papers are roughly in the order in which they were received. Minimal re-arrangement was performed within individual boxes. Personal and family papers can also be found within Series I. Writings and Series II. Professional correspondence and paprers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal and family papers consist of family correspondence, writings of family members, scrapbooks, diaries, notebooks, photographs, yearbooks, realia, and other personal papers documenting Merrill Joan Gerber's family life and history.

Henry W. Sherman personal effects from World War II plane crash

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Henry William Sherman was Merrill Joan Gerber's cousin. A First Lieutenant in the U.S. Army during the Second World War, Sherman was co-pilot of the "Early Delivery," a C-47-DL, when it crashed on February 6, 1943, near Uau, New Guinea, following a raid by Japanese fighter planes. The plane was lost at the time, but the wreckage was discovered in 1988. Sherman's remains were recovered and buried in Arlington National Cemetary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Henry Sherman's personal effects recovered from the wreckage of the "Early Delivery" in 1988 include eye glasses, belt buckle, knife, and two Australian coins. They are accompanied by his Distinguished Flying Cross medal, a commemorative matchbook with Sherman's photograph, and a framed photograph of Sherman's gravestone from Arlington National Cemetary.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Beinecke Library staff opened and emptied the vial containing water from the Grand Canal in Venice on April 20, 2021. The other vials were empty when received.
